text: **Anna:** Tonight I am teaching my {ukulele|ウクレレ/小型ギター|/ˌjuːkəˈleɪli/|I play ukulele every weekend.} class.
**Anna:** It is {far|遠い/はるか遠く} away, so I am going to ride the {metro|地下鉄|/ˈmetrəʊ/|The metro is fast in big cities.}.
**Anna:** I lost my {wallet|財布|/ˈwɒlɪt/|My wallet is in my bag.}!
**Anna:** It has my Metro card, my {credit card|クレジットカード} and my money!
**Anna:** I remember my father's {important|重要な/大切な} words.
**Anna:** Always have {emergency|緊急の/非常時の} money.
**Anna:** Anna, it's time for Plan C
**Anna:** I'm {stuck|立ち往生した/身動きが取れない} downtown without any money.
**Anna:** Would you be able to come downtown, please?
**Marsha:** I have to give a big {presentation|プレゼンテーション/発表} in one hour.
**Jonathan:** Things are {great|素晴らしい/上手く行っている|/ɡreɪt/|We had a great time together.}
**Anna:** Well, I was {wondering|考えている/疑問に思っている|/ˈwʌndərɪŋ/|I was wondering about the answer.} if you could give me

**Anna:** See, I {lost|なくした/失った|/lɔːst/|I lost my keys yesterday.} my {wallet|財布|/ˈwɒlɪt/|My wallet is in my bag.} and I'm {stuck|立ち往生した/動けなくなった|/stʌk/|The car got stuck in mud.} downtown and I Uh Anna, I wish I could.
**Anna:** I'm at the {airport|空港|/ˈɛrpɔːrt/|We arrived at the airport early.} with my mother
**Anna:** Her flight {leaves|出発する|/liːvz/|The train leaves at 3 pm.} in two hours.
**Anna:** Tell your mom to have a nice trip.
**Jonathan:** I do wish I could help.
**Anna:** This is {serious|深刻な/真剣な|/ˈsɪəriəs/|This is a serious problem.}
**Anna:** {Whatcha|何をしている（口語）|/ˈwɒtʃə/|Whatcha doing this weekend?} doing?
**Anna:** Uh, Ashley, I was wondering, uh, would you be able to come downtown and could you give me $20?
**Anna:** I'll pay you back the money, I {promise|約束する|/ˈprɒmɪs/|I promise to help you.}
**Anna:** Anna, I'm not worried about the money.
**Jonathan:** I'm {babysitting|子守りをしている|/ˈbeɪbɪsɪtɪŋ/|I'm babysitting my nephew today.}
**Anna:** Well, losing your wallet is bad, but it's not the end of the world, Ashley.
**Ashley:** I was talking to my {niece|姪|/niːs/|My niece is very smart.}, Anna.
**Ashley:** Good luck {getting|手に入れる/獲得する|/ˈɡetɪŋ/|I'm getting a new book.} money.
**Anna:** Good luck {babysitting|子守り/子供の世話|/ˈbeɪbɪˌsɪtɪŋ/|She enjoys babysitting her nephew.}
**Anna:** Well, plans A, B, C, D, and E did not {work|機能する/成功する|/wɜːrk/|The plan did not work today.}
**Anna:** Think time for plan F {won't|〜しないだろう|/wənt/|He won't come today.} you give?
**Anna:** Could you thank you very much, sir?
**Anna:** Would you {give|与える/渡す|/ɡɪv/|Please give me a pen.} me money?
**Anna:** Till next time, won't you give me money?
